mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the equity discount rate and supports valuation multiples, so month-ahead S&P 500 returns are positive when the policy rate is still easing. The signal is confirmed if 20-day returns improve after sustained rate cuts and fails if cuts do not coincide with stronger index performance.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months where FEDFUNDS declines over the prior 3 months, or if rolling 3-year IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE marks deteriorating labor income and weaker demand, which compresses expected corporate sales and profits. Once unemployment rises fast enough, equity returns over the next month should turn negative.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 45% of the time, or if the average triggered return is not below unconditional mean by at least 1 standard error.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with falling UNRATE signals synchronized real activity improvement that lifts cyclical earnings expectations and risk appetite. The joint condition should predict positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more strongly than either series alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power over single-series effects, or if the joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in more than half of matched instances.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rise in RSAFS indicates improving consumer and business spending, which feeds revenue growth expectations and supports equity prices over the next week. The signal is strongest when sales momentum is broad rather than isolated to one month.
failure_condition: Reject if months with RSAFS gains above 1.0% do not outperform the unconditional 5-day return distribution, or if the hit rate is below 52% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL acceleration exceeds the policy rate, real rates become more negative and financial conditions loosen in real terms, supporting near-term equity returns. The mechanism fails if inflation acceleration does not coincide with higher forward returns or if the market interprets it as a tightening shock.
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-above-policy regime does not yield higher 20-day SPX returns than the opposite regime, or if the sign flips negative for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A downturn in HOUST after a prior rise signals weakening housing demand and a broader slowdown in rate-sensitive consumption and investment. That deterioration should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if large housing declines are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the average post-trigger return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous deterioration in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE captures labor market slack that undermines household income and corporate revenue expectations. This combined labor-stress regime should predict negative returns over the next quarter more reliably than either series alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-UNRATE combination does not outperform single-variable models, or if 60-day forward returns are not below baseline in at least 60% of stressed observations.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession rebound, rising INDPRO and PAYEMS indicate rebuilding output and payrolls, which sustains earnings revisions and risk appetite over the next 60 trading days. The signal is positive only when both activity and hiring are improving together after a prior contraction.
failure_condition: Reject if joint rises in INDPRO and PAYEMS are not associated with positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the recovery-stage hit rate falls below 55% in rolling out-of-sample tests.
